actual.name: actual
nodes: wandering AI agents across all domains
actual.summary: A system that receives a user's incomplete intellectual state — current pursuit, unfinished inspirations, and vague vision — and uses that three-point anchor to send AI agents wandering unpredictably across all knowledge domains, retrieving structurally resonant work (published research, concepts, metaphors) that can either validate, extend, or crystallize the user's half-formed idea into something livable and advanceable.

search_queries: minimum viable input for creative systems
node_records.embedding_text: a minimal but sufficient input bundle — current work, undone threads, and mental image — that gives a system enough structural grip to act without requiring full articulation from the user

node_records.text: wandering AI agents across all domains
node_records.embedding_text: autonomous agents traversing unconstrained knowledge space with no fixed domain boundary, following structural resonance rather than topical similarity
building forward from existing published research or concepts by detecting structural lineage and identifying where the next extension lives
a system that converts a user's inarticulate mental image into a structured, expressible concept by finding external analogues that mirror its shape
movement through knowledge space that cannot be predicted forward but can be reconstructed backward — the trail is real, the destination is not pre-known
retrieval that returns both literal matches in the user's own field and structurally equivalent analogues from entirely unrelated domains — math, theater, biology, literature

essence.name: essence
essence.summary: The structural tension here is between incompleteness and generativity — the user's idea is deliberately half-formed, and that half-formedness is not a defect to be patched but the productive raw material the system runs on. The system must hold the anchor firm enough to score relevance while keeping the search chaotic enough to surface non-obvious resonances. This is bounded chaos: the anchor does not predict the destination, it only judges the catch. The deeper tension is between articulation and discovery — if the user could fully articulate the idea, they wouldn't need the system; the system's job is to make the unarticulated visible by finding its reflection elsewhere.

node_records.embedding_text: the half-formed state of an idea is not a gap to be filled before work begins but the active ingredient that makes generative search possible
a fixed reference point that scores relevance after the fact without constraining the path taken to get there — judgment without direction
a system that is fully unconstrained in its wandering but constrained in what counts as a valid find — freedom of motion, strictness of return
the user's idea becomes expressible not by internal refinement but by encountering its structural mirror in an external domain — seeing the shape from outside
relevance is determined by shared dynamic shape — the same tension, cycle, or asymmetry — not by shared vocabulary, field, or surface subject matter
a bare structural outline — without flesh, without full specification — carries enough information to anchor a search and evaluate its returns

mechanism.name: mechanism
nodes: recognition precedes articulation — exploit the asymmetry
the anchor is a skeleton, not a map
chaos produces surface area; anchor filters signal
past is trackable, future is not — and that is the point
half-knowledge as structural input is sufficient
mechanism.summary: The causal primitive operating here is: systems tasked with advancing underspecified creative or intellectual states cannot use deterministic search because the user does not yet know what they are looking for — only whether they recognize it when they see it. This means the search must be genuinely stochastic (chaotic traversal, Markov-style next-hop independence) while the evaluation is anchored (the three-point input bundle acts as a scoring skeleton, not a search directive). The mechanism is recognition-before-articulation: the user's brain can evaluate a match before it can generate a description of what it was matching against. The system exploits this asymmetry — generate widely, score against the skeleton, surface the resonant catch. The Heisenberg parallel is exact: observing (scoring) does not require predicting (directing). The path is unknowable forward; the relevance is knowable backward.

node_records.text: recognition precedes articulation — exploit the asymmetry
node_records.embedding_text: the human capacity to recognize a correct match far outpaces the capacity to describe what is being matched against — systems can exploit this gap by generating candidates for recognition rather than requiring prior specification
the path through a search space must be non-deterministic to surface non-obvious results, but the scoring function must be fixed — randomness in motion, strictness in judgment

node_records.text: the anchor is a skeleton, not a map
node_records.embedding_text: a structural input bundle tells the system what shape a valid answer must have, not where to look for it — navigation is free, recognition criteria are fixed

node_records.text: chaos produces surface area; anchor filters signal
node_records.embedding_text: unpredictable traversal maximizes the variety of candidates encountered; the anchor then collapses that variety into a small set of structurally resonant returns — chaos generates, structure selects

node_records.text: past is trackable, future is not — and that is the point
node_records.embedding_text: a system designed for discovery must resist the urge to predict its own path; the value is in the surprise of the destination, not the efficiency of the route

node_records.text: half-knowledge as structural input is sufficient
node_records.embedding_text: a system does not need complete specification to begin — a partial structural skeleton carries enough constraint to make evaluation possible without collapsing the search space prematurely

problem.name: problem
problem.content: I'm currently working on a system that helps people advance their concepts or the work they're currently working on. If there's a case or research that's already published, based on that inheritance, based on that work, we can advance it. If I'm the one who didn't want to be at the same level and I'm just having some similar inspirations and I wanted to take that method or research paper or concept to a next level — how can I do this? I'm making the system like that. Or I'm having a whole new concept: if there's a system I'm building and I wanted to make a feature out of it and I'm having some vague inspirations of that system but I don't know how to articulate it, I don't know how to make it live — there's a system that can do that part of it.

vision.name: vision
vision.content: If a person tells the inspiration, the pursuit, and any unfinished work or unfinished threads in their mind — some of the big inspirations — to the model, then the model has something to anchor against. These three information points — the current pursuit (whether half-baked or not), the undone inspirations, and the vision the user is actually visualizing in their brain — can give enough data to the model to keep intact its score and keep wandering around through the Internet to find native and metaphorical work in the respective domains, whether it's math, physics, psychology, or even theater, literature, anything.

current_map.name: current_map
current_map.content: Based on what I told you about this system, you can't be working on it without some theoretical seeds. I stumbled into many concepts — Heisenberg, Markov chain, chaos theory. Chaos theory is what lets the model wander the Internet unhindered, like total chaos. There's no prediction of where the model will go next, but we can track it. But that track is the past, not the future. We cannot predict where it will end up. That is the spontaneousness I want to give to the model. The Markov chain also plays an important role in my thinking. The same goes with the Heisenberg principle — it doesn't provide a clear understanding, but a half-understanding. Just a structure. Just a skeleton.
